candidates.row_label: Depreciation of property, plant and equipment
candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line, but nothing in the excerpt links it specifically to the selected COGS bucket ('Cost of goods and services sold' / 'Goods for resale' / 'Subcontractors and subscriptions'). Conservatively avoid assigning general operating D&A to COGS without clear COGS labeling.
Separate operating amortization line, but not identified as cost-of-sales/COGS related. Adding it to COGS would be speculative and risks misclassification.
Looks like a more specific amortization component rather than a clearly COGS-linked D&A row; also may overlap with broader amortization. Not appropriate for separate COGS_DA addition.

variables.notes: No explicit depreciation/amortization row is labeled as belonging to COGS/cost of sales. The visible D&A rows are separate operating lines, but their bucket attribution is not clearly COGS. Conservative choice is to add none to COGS_DA.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation of property, plant and equipment
candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A line above operating profit and outside the selected XSGA component rows ('Other external expenses' and 'Personnel expenses'). Since prior XSGA mapping intentionally excluded depreciation/amortization, this should be added separately to XSGA-related operating overhead.

Explicit separate amortization line above operating profit and outside the selected XSGA component rows. It appears at the same operating-expense level and should be added separately rather than assumed embedded in the selected XSGA rows.

Likely a sub-component of the broader 'Amortizationofintangiblefixedassets' row, especially given both appear in the same operating section. Exclude to avoid double counting under the summary-vs-components rule.

final_choice.row_label: Depreciation of property, plant and equipment

variables.notes: Selected only the separate operating D&A rows that sit outside the chosen XSGA component bucket. Excluded 'Acquisition-relatedamortization' as a likely component of the broader amortization row. Ignored 'Depreciation, amortization and impairment' because it mixes D&A with impairment and would risk contamination/double counting.
